nodes.x: My son, if thou wilt receive my words, and hide my commandments with thee;
My son, keep my words, and lay up my commandments with thee.
My son, give ear to my words; for I swear unto you, that inasmuch as ye shall keep the commandments of God ye shall prosper in the land.

My son, give ear to my words, for I say unto you, even as I said unto Helaman, that inasmuch as ye shall keep the commandments of God ye shall prosper in the land; and inasmuch as ye will not keep the commandments of God ye shall be cut off from his presence.
Now therefore, my son, obey my voice according to that which I command thee.
¶ My son, keep thy father’s commandment, and forsake not the law of thy mother:
So that thou incline thine ear unto wisdom, and apply thine heart to understanding;
¶ When wisdom entereth into thine heart, and knowledge is pleasant unto thy soul;
¶ Trust in the Lord with all thine heart; and lean not unto thine own understanding.
My son, attend unto my wisdom, and bow thine ear to my understanding:
Hear thou, my son, and be wise, and guide thine heart in the way.
So shall the knowledge of wisdom be unto thy soul: when thou hast found it, then there shall be a reward, and thy expectation shall not be cut off.
Yea, if thou criest after knowledge, and liftest up thy voice for understanding;
Let my cry come near before thee, O Lord: give me understanding according to thy word.
If now thou hast understanding, hear this: hearken to the voice of my words.
But continue thou in the things which thou hast learned and hast been assured of, knowing of whom thou hast learned them;
And to another, that he may teach the word of knowledge by the same Spirit;
What knowest thou, that we know not? what understandest thou, which is not in us?
If thou seekest her as silver, and searchest for her as for hid treasures;
With thy wisdom and with thine understanding thou hast gotten thee riches, and hast gotten gold and silver into thy treasures:
¶ Either what woman having ten pieces of silver, if she lose one piece, doth not light a candle, and sweep the house, and seek diligently till she find it?

"We will go to distant lands, where no one has ever heard our name. _I
will buy an estate where we can live in comfort._ I may become as rich
as I please; look in this niche here; _here are treasures heaped up that
we need only to take; all is mine_. It was left me as an inheritance by
the one who hid it here in former days. I have the proof in writing. The
treasure is doubly mine; on the casks…
Your gold and silver is cankered; and the rust of them shall be a witness against you, and shall eat your flesh as it were fire. Ye have heaped treasure together for the last days.
Then shalt thou understand the fear of the Lord, and find the knowledge of God.
Then hear thou from the heavens, even from thy dwelling place, their prayer and their supplications, and maintain their cause, and forgive thy people which have sinned against thee.
And seek the face of the Lord always, that in patience ye may possess your souls, and ye shall have eternal life.
Be strong and of a good courage, fear not, nor be afraid of them: for the Lord thy God, he it is that doth go with thee; he will not fail thee, nor forsake thee.
Then shalt thou inquire, and make search, and ask diligently; and, behold, if it be truth, and the thing certain, that such abomination is wrought among you;
Then hear thou in heaven their prayer and their supplication, and maintain their cause.
For the Lord giveth wisdom: out of his mouth cometh knowledge and understanding.
Whence then cometh wisdom? and where is the place of understanding?
Wisdom is the principal thing; therefore get wisdom: and with all thy getting get understanding.
But where shall wisdom be found? and where is the place of understanding?
Now, behold this is wisdom; whoso readeth, let him understand and receive also;
A scorner seeketh wisdom, and findeth it not: but knowledge is easy unto him that understandeth.
He layeth up sound wisdom for the righteous: he is a buckler to them that walk uprightly.
He that walketh uprightly, and worketh righteousness, and speaketh the truth in his heart.
He that followeth after righteousness and mercy findeth life, righteousness, and honour.
Unto the upright there ariseth light in the darkness: he is gracious, and full of compassion, and righteous.
The highway of the upright is to depart from evil: he that keepeth his way preserveth his soul.

For behold, he has his friends in iniquity, and he keepeth his guards about him; and he teareth up the laws of those who have reigned in righteousness before him; and he trampleth under his feet the commandments of God;
He keepeth the paths of judgment, and preserveth the way of his saints.

known to his saints necessary for their salvation; and as he possesses the attribute power he is able thereby to deliver them from the power of all enemies; and seeing also, that justice is an attribute of the Deity, he will deal with them upon the principles of righteousness and equity, and a just reward will be granted unto them for all their afflictions and sufferings for the truth’s sake. And…

and preserves in life whomsoever he sees proper, and takes away his servants whenever it seems to him good to do so. Whether I shall be spared many years or few, it matters not to me, if I am only faithful to the end. This is my object; this is the foremost thing in my mind; and it should be the foremost in the minds of all the Latter-day Saints. Many great and good men have fallen by the power…
He that keepeth the commandment keepeth his own soul; but he that despiseth his ways shall die.
For the Lord loveth judgment, and forsaketh not his saints; they are preserved for ever: but the seed of the wicked shall be cut off.
Then shalt thou understand righteousness, and judgment, and equity; yea, every good path.
That thou mayest walk in the way of good men, and keep the paths of the righteous.
So shalt thou find favour and good understanding in the sight of God and man.
All the paths of the Lord are mercy and truth unto such as keep his covenant and his testimonies.
Then hear thou in heaven, and do, and judge thy servants, condemning the wicked, to bring his way upon his head; and justifying the righteous, to give him according to his righteousness.
But judgment shall return unto righteousness: and all the upright in heart shall follow it.
¶ When wisdom entereth into thine heart, and knowledge is pleasant unto thy soul;
So that thou incline thine ear unto wisdom, and apply thine heart to understanding;
He that getteth wisdom loveth his own soul: he that keepeth understanding shall find good.
Wisdom is the olive which springeth from the heart, bloometh on the tongue, and beareth fruit in the actions.
The heart of the prudent getteth knowledge; and the ear of the wise seeketh knowledge.
Discretion shall preserve thee, understanding shall keep thee:
That thou mayest regard discretion, and that thy lips may keep knowledge.
Forsake her not, and she shall preserve thee: love her, and she shall keep thee.
Beware that thou forget not the Lord thy God, in not keeping his commandments, and his judgments, and his statutes, which I command thee this day:

And, behold, I am with thee, and will keep thee in all places whither thou goest, and will bring thee again into this land; for I will not leave thee, until I have done that which I have spoken to thee of.
Hold thou me up, and I shall be safe: and I will have respect unto thy statutes continually.
To deliver thee from the way of the evil man, from the man that speaketh froward things;
Behold, here is the man, we deliver him into thy hands; thou mayest do with him as seemeth thee good.
Delivering thee from the people, and from the Gentiles, unto whom now I send thee,
And the devil said unto him, All this power will I give thee, and the glory of them: for that is delivered unto me; and to whomsoever I will I give it.
And I will deliver thee out of the hand of the wicked, and I will redeem thee out of the hand of the terrible.
And when thou deliveredst up that which God had given thee sight and power to translate, thou deliveredst up that which was sacred into the hands of a wicked man,
Who leave the paths of uprightness, to walk in the ways of darkness;
With his strong hand he stayed the powers of darkness — he provided a path f r our feet, and led us forth from the gales of death!
The paths of their way are turned aside; they go to nothing, and perish.
But if a man walk in the night, he stumbleth, because there is no light in him.

And it came to pass that there arose a mist of darkness; yea, even an exceedingly great mist of darkness, insomuch that they who had commenced in the path did lose their way, that they wandered off and were lost.
Who rejoice to do evil, and delight in the frowardness of the wicked;
The wicked shall be a ransom for the righteous, and the transgressor for the upright.
The desire of the righteous is only good: but the expectation of the wicked is wrath.
There shall no evil happen to the just: but the wicked shall be filled with mischief.
The way of the wicked is an abomination unto the Lord: but he loveth him that followeth after righteousness.
Evil shall slay the wicked: and they that hate the righteous shall be desolate.
Whose ways are crooked, and they froward in their paths:
That which is crooked cannot be made straight: and that which is wanting cannot be numbered.
Consider the work of God: for who can make that straight, which he hath made crooked?

For God doth not walk in crooked paths, neither doth he turn to the right hand nor to the left, neither doth he vary from that which he hath said, therefore his paths are straight, and his course is one eternal round.

“T gave you,” he said, “ directions in the first 
commandment to attend to faith, and fear, and 
self-restraint.” ‘‘ Even so, sir,” said I. And he 
said, ‘Now I wish to show you the powers of 
these, that you may know what power each pos- 
sesses. For their powers are double, and have 
relation alike to the righteous and the unright- 
eous. ‘Trust you, therefore, the righteous, but 
put no trust…
To deliver thee from the strange woman, even from the stranger which flattereth with her words;
That they may keep thee from the strange woman, from the stranger which flattereth with her words.
To keep thee from the evil woman, from the flattery of the tongue of a strange woman.
To deliver thee from the way of the evil man, from the man that speaketh froward things;
Not to many people of a strange speech and of an hard language, whose words thou canst not understand. Surely, had I sent thee to them, they would have hearkened unto thee.
Which forsaketh the guide of her youth, and forgetteth the covenant of her God.
To such as keep his covenant, and to those that remember his commandments to do them.
¶ Nevertheless I will remember my covenant with thee in the days of thy youth, and I will establish unto thee an everlasting covenant.
¶ Yet ye say, Wherefore? Because the Lord hath been witness between thee and the wife of thy youth, against whom thou hast dealt treacherously: yet is she thy companion, and the wife of thy covenant.
¶ For the sins of her prophets, and the iniquities of her priests, that have shed the blood of the just in the midst of her,
For her house inclineth unto death, and her paths unto the dead.
And her gates shall lament and mourn; and she shall be desolate, and shall sit upon the ground.
And her gates shall lament and mourn; and she being desolate shall sit upon the ground.
Her house is the way to hell, going down to the chambers of death.

And toward her young one that cometh out from between her feet, and toward her children which she shall bear: for she shall eat them for want of all things secretly in the siege and straitness, wherewith thine enemy shall distress thee in thy gates.
Therefore shall her plagues come in one day, death, and mourning, and famine; and she shall be utterly burned with fire: for strong is the Lord God who judgeth her.
None that go unto her return again, neither take they hold of the paths of life.
And they said unto her, Surely we will return with thee unto thy people.
For her house inclineth unto death, and her paths unto the dead.

And she shall follow after her lovers, but she shall not overtake them; and she shall seek them, but shall not find them: then shall she say, I will go and return to my first husband; for then was it better with me than now.
Lest thou shouldest ponder the path of life, her ways are moveable, that thou canst not know them.
That thou mayest walk in the way of good men, and keep the paths of the righteous.
That the Lord thy God may shew us the way wherein we may walk, and the thing that we may do.
Therefore thou shalt keep the commandments of the Lord thy God, to walk in his ways, and to fear him.

May the eternal God make thy path straight, And the Lord [(be) with thee, and] protect thee from all evil, And grant unto thee grace, mercy and favour before those who see thee, And may none of the children of men have power over thee to harm thee; Go in peace.
That he may incline our hearts unto him, to walk in all his ways, and to keep his commandments, and his statutes, and his judgments, which he commanded our fathers.
But thou, O man of God, flee these things; and follow after righteousness, godliness, faith, love, patience, meekness.
For the upright shall dwell in the land, and the perfect shall remain in it.
Thou wast perfect in thy ways from the day that thou wast created, till iniquity was found in thee.
The righteous shall inherit the land, and dwell therein for ever.
Be ye therefore perfect, even as your Father which is in heaven is perfect.
And ye shall dwell with us: and the land shall be before you; dwell and trade ye therein, and get you possessions therein.
The righteous shall be glad in the Lord, and shall trust in him; and all the upright in heart shall glory.
But the wicked shall be cut off from the earth, and the transgressors shall be rooted out of it.
But the transgressors shall be destroyed together: the end of the wicked shall be cut off.
The righteous shall never be removed: but the wicked shall not inhabit the earth.
That it might take hold of the ends of the earth, that the wicked might be shaken out of it?
The wicked shall be turned into hell, and all the nations that forget God.
